body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` **Service directory:** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/` **Triggered by:** PR #58031 by @jsquire ## What Changed PR #58031 added a new `README.md` for `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` as part of a CodeChecks validation fix. The README covers core agent workflows and several tool integrations, but 35 sample files exist in `samples/` and over a dozen of the newer ones (added in beta.7 through beta.10) have no README coverage. ## Gaps Found The following capabilities have dedicated sample `.md` files but are **not mentioned anywhere in the README's Examples section**: - **Streaming** — `Sample9_PersistentAgents_Streaming.md` and `Sample20_PersistentAgents_FileSearch_Steaming.md` - **Image inputs** — `Sample17_PersistentAgents_ImageUrlInputs.md` and `Sample18_PersistentAgents_ImageFileInputs.md` - **Vector Store Batch File Search** — `Sample10_PersistentAgents_Vector_Store_Batch_File_Search.md` and `Sample15_PersistentAgents_Vector_Store_Batch_Enterprise_File_Search.md` - **VectorStore with Steps** — `Sample19_PersistentAgents_VectorStoreFile_WithSteps.md` - **SharePoint integration** — `Sample21_PersistentAgents_Sharepoint.md` - **Microsoft Fabric integration** — `Sample22_PersistentAgents_Fabric.md` - **Connected Agents** — `Sample23_PersistentAgents_Connected_Agent.md` and `Sample24_PersistentAgents_Multiple_Connected_Agents.md` - **Bing Custom Search** — `Sample25_PersistentAgents_Bing_Custom_Search.md` - **IChatClient integration** — `Sample26_PersistentAgents_As_IChatClient.md` - **MCP (Model Context Protocol)** — `Sample27_PersistentAgents_MCP_Streaming.md` and `Sample32_PersistentAgents_MCP.md` - **Bing Grounding Streaming** — `Sample28_PersistentAgents_Bing_Grounding_streaming.md` - **Browser Automation Tool** — `Sample29_PersistentAgents_BrowserAutomationTool.md` - **Streaming with Auto Function Calls** — `Sample30_Streaming_with_Auto_Function_Calls.md` - **Deep Research** — `Sample31_PersistentAgents_DeepResearch.md` - **Computer Use** — `Sample33_Computer_Use.md` - **Logging / diagnostics** — `Sample34_PersistentAgents_Logging.md` (only briefly mentioned in Troubleshooting; no code example) - **Structured Outputs** — `Sample35_PersistentAgents_StructuredOutputs.md` The highest-priority gaps are the recently-added features (beta.8–beta.10): **Computer Use**, **MCP**, **Connected Agents**, **Deep Research**, and **Structured Outputs** — these represent significant new capabilities with no README coverage.
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **Edit:** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/README.md` 1. Expand the **Table of Contents** under `- [Examples](#examples)` to add entries for each new subsection: ```markdown - [Streaming](#streaming) - [Image inputs](#image-inputs) - [Connected Agents](#connected-agents) - [MCP (Model Context Protocol)](#mcp-model-context-protocol) - [Computer Use](#computer-use) - [Deep Research](#deep-research) - [Structured Outputs](#structured-outputs) - [Bing Custom Search](#bing-custom-search) - [SharePoint](#sharepoint) - [Microsoft Fabric](#microsoft-fabric) - [Browser Automation](#browser-automation) - [Streaming with Auto Function Calls](#streaming-with-auto-function-calls) - [IChatClient integration](#ichatclient-integration) - [Logging](#logging) ``` 2. After the `#### Create Agent With OpenAPI` section (around line 944), add new `####`-level sections for each missing feature. Each section should briefly explain the feature and include a snippet placeholder. For example: ```markdown #### Computer Use The `ComputerUseToolDefinition` allows an agent to interact with a desktop computer environment: ```C# Snippet:ComputerUseCreateAgent ``` ``` Use the same pattern for Connected Agents, Deep Research, Structured Outputs, MCP, and the remaining features. ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets Locate the actual `#region Snippet:` names used in each test file under `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/tests/Samples/` and use those exact names in the README placeholders. The `Update-Snippets.ps1` script will inject the actual code automatically. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README already has all six required top-level sections (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing). The work here is purely additive — expanding the **Examples** section. Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next. The snippet script modifies files in-place — those changes must be included in the commit. 1. `dotnet build sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 ai` — injects snippet code from test files into README placeholders; verify `git diff` shows README updated 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 ai` — only needed if any public API changed (not expected for documentation-only work) 5. `dotnet format sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
title: [AI Agents] Docs: README missing coverage for newer features (MCP, Computer Use, Connected Agents, Deep Research, Structured Outputs, and more)
